div.p: Both parent AND child must call setpgid() to guarantee correct process group membership
Race Window: Child execs before parent calls setpgid() Child remains in shell's process group → receives signals meant for shell!
Race Eliminated: Whichever runs first (parent or child) creates the process group. Second call is harmless no-op → group already exists.

text.tspan: // Put child in its own process group
// Use child's PID as the new PGID
// EACCES is OK: child already set the group
// ALSO call setpgid to win the race
// Now safe to exec - we're in correct group
// Put child in its own process group
// Use child's PID as the new PGID
// EACCES is OK: child already set the group
// ALSO call setpgid to win the race
// Now safe to exec - we're in correct group

ul.li: An attempt is made to change the process group ID of one of the children of the calling process and the child has already performed an execve() In our case: If the child's setpgid(0,0) ran first and the child has already called execvp(), the parent's setpgid(pid, pid) will return -1 with errno = EACCES. This is NOT an error — the group was already established by the child! We only fail on other errors (EPERM, ESRCH, EINVAL).
